title: Apply: The 27 Amendments
description: Apply your understanding of the 27 Amendments to the Constitution.
topic_path: Civil Dialogue, The Constitution, and Constitutional Interpretation

assessment_items.question_content:
Anya, a reporter for the *Milwaukee Journal,* has found evidence that the mayor has committed some serious crimes. The day before the story goes to print, the police commissioner tells her that if she publishes the story, he’ll arrest her for harassment. She publishes it anyway.

**What right protects Anya's publication of the story?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Evan is driving home on his motorcycle when a police officer turns on his lights to pull him over. Afraid he’ll get a ticket, Evan speeds away. Despite driving very fast, the police officer catches up to Evan and forces him to pull over. Evan is arrested for resisting arrest and trying to interfere with a police investigation and goes on trial a  few days later. The prosecutor wants Evan to testify and tell the jury the truth about what happened.

**Which amendment protects Evan from having to testify against himself?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Paula has a house on the coast of Oregon. The government wants to build a highway along the coast, but to do that they would need to take a significant amount of Paula’s property. 

**Which amendment says that Paula must receive appropriate payment before the government can take her property to build a highway?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Maria and Don are in a dispute over a quilt. Maria is from Iowa, and Don is from California. Don made the quilt for Maria after she ordered it on his website. Don charged her \$200. However, upon receiving the quilt, Maria refused to pay. She said the blanket she received was not the one she ordered. 

**Which amendment guarantees that Don and Maria can have a jury trial to settle this dispute?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

In 1917, three-fourths of states ratified an amendment prohibiting the manufacture, sale, and transportation of alcohol, resulting in the Eighteenth Amendment to the United States Constitution. However, there is no longer any restriction on the manufacture, sale, and transportation of alcohol. In fact, there are more than 65,000 bars in the United States today.

**Which amendment effectively led to this change in policy?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

One of the chief complaints in the Declaration of Independence was that the British crown forced colonists to provide free housing for soldiers.

**Which amendment protects Americans from the government doing this again?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Before 1800, the candidate with the second most votes in a presidential election became the vice president.  This changed after the election of 1800. Following a constitutional amendment, electors would cast two votes: one for president and one for vice president. 

**Which amendment caused this change?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

In their annual budget, the House of Representatives proposes an increase in the tax rate for people who make more than \$250,000 per year from 35\% to 37.5\%. Devin is in this tax bracket and affected by this change. He wants to protest this increase.

**If Devin asked you whether Congress has the power to make this change, which amendment would you show them?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**This is an excerpt from the New York Declaration of Rights, Form of Ratification, and Recommendatory Amendments to the Constitution (1788). This document includes the amendments that New York proposed at the state ratifying convention for the U.S. Constitution.**

That no Person shall be eligible to the Office of President of the United States a third time.

**This proposed amendment was not adopted in the Bill of Rights, but its concerns were later addressed in which amendment?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**This is an excerpt from the Northwest Ordinance (1787).**

All persons shall be bailable, unless for [[☃ definition 1]] offenses, where the proof shall be evident or the presumption great. All fines shall be moderate; and no cruel or unusual punishments shall be inflicted. 

**The protections listed in this text are echoed in which amendment?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**This is an excerpt from the Vermont Declaration of Rights (1777).**

That in all prosecutions for criminal offenses, a man has a right to be heard by himself and his [[☃ definition 1]],—to demand the cause and nature of his accusation,—to be confronted with the witnesses,—to call for evidence in his favor, and a speedy public trial by an impartial jury of the country, without the unanimous consent of which jury he cannot be found guilty:—nor can he be [[☃ definition 2]] to give evidence against himself:—nor can any man be justly deprived of his liberty, except by the laws of the land, or the judgment of his Peers.

**The protections listed in this text are echoed in which __TWO__ amendments?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**This is an excerpt from the Massachusetts Preamble and Declaration of Rights (1780).**

The people of this commonwealth have the sole and exclusive right of governing themselves as a free, sovereign, and independent state; and do, and for ever hereafter shall, exercise and enjoy every power, jurisdiction, and right, which is not, or may not hereafter, be by them expressly delegated to the United States of America, in Congress assembled.

**The protections listed in this text are echoed in which amendment?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
